      Open

      Fix false "Ranks do not match sort condition" error after swapParent with active sort#4016
      Copilot wants to merge 4 commits intomaincybersemics/em:mainfrom
      copilot/fix-red-sort-icon-when-swapping-parentcybersemics/em:copilot/fix-red-sort-icon-when-swapping-parentCopy head branch name to clipboard

      Conversation

      Sorry, something went wrong.

      Uh oh!

      There was an error while loading. Please reload this page.
      View repo at this time
      Contributor

      Copilot AI commented Apr 1, 2026 •
      edited
      Loading

      Uh oh!

      There was an error while loading. Please reload this page.

      After swapParent, the =sort meta-attribute (a child of the original parent) migrates to the new parent as a sibling. This causes the new parent to inherit the sort preference, but since its children's ranks were not updated to reflect that sort order, the rank-vs-sorted comparison in toggleSortPicker incorrectly triggers the red Sort icon error.

      Changes

      • src/actions/swapParent.ts — After all move operations, call sort(childId) to rerank the new parent's children according to any active sort preference. This is a no-op when no sort preference is active:
      // If an active sort preference exists on the new parent (e.g. =sort migrated from the old parent
// as a sibling), rerank its children to match the sort order and prevent a false rank mismatch error.
sort(childId),
      • src/actions/__tests__/swapParent.ts — Added test reproducing the exact scenario: import A with Alphabetical sort and child B, swap B with A, place cursor on B (now at root), and assert via exportContext that B's children are in the correct sorted order (=sort before a, as meta-attributes sort first in Alphabetical order).
      Copilot uses AI. Check for mistakes.

      No, there's a real problem here. Metaprogramming attributes are sortable, and they're part of the compareReasonable order.

      Contributor Author

      Copilot AI commented Apr 1, 2026

      Choose a reason for hiding this comment

      The reason will be displayed to describe this comment to others. Learn more about hiding comments.

      No, there's a real problem here. Metaprogramming attributes are sortable, and they're part of the compareReasonable order.

      Reverted that approach. The real fix is in swapParent.ts: after all the moves, sort(childId) is called to rerank the new parent's children to match any active sort preference (it's a no-op when no sort is active). This keeps meta-attributes in the sort comparison and ensures ranks always match the sort condition. (4d8ebda)

          raineorshine Apr 2, 2026

          There was a problem hiding this comment.

          Choose a reason for hiding this comment

          The reason will be displayed to describe this comment to others. Learn more.

          This incorrectly places =sort as a child of a instead of a root child. It's the root that is sorted.

          Updates Steps to Reproduce:

          - A
- C
- D

          Step to Reproduce

          1. Set cursor on A
          2. Tap Sort and select Created
          3. Create Subthought B under A
          4. Set cursor on B
          5. Tap Swap Parent
          6. Set Cursor on A

          Current behavior

          Sort button is red.
          Sorting is not direction sensitive, both direction can reproduce this error

          Red.Sort.icon.when.swapping.parent.while.Created.Sorting.is.active.-.Updated.mov

          Expected behavior

          Sort button should remain white.
          Refresh and try again.
